RDW.headline: US Stocks Show AI Breadth Is Replacing Defensive Leadership
RDW.description: US equities rallied post-Memorial Day with strong AI and small-cap leadership, as de-escalation hopes in the US-Iran conflict drove oil prices lower. Micron Technology surged 18% to cross $1 trillion market cap on a UBS price target hike, while quantum computing and space stocks rallied on government funding and SpaceX IPO anticipation. The Nasdaq rose 1.11% and Russell 2000 jumped 1.74%, though the Dow declined 0.19% as defensive sectors unwound.

RDW.headline: Space Stocks Are Flying Ahead Of NASA Moon Base Event, SpaceX IPO
RDW.description: Space stocks rallied on Tuesday as investors positioned ahead of NASA's Moon Base press conference and digested SpaceX's recent S-1 IPO filing. The momentum is being driven by expectations of government-backed space spending, lunar infrastructure contracts, and renewed investor appetite for space sector names. Key beneficiaries include lunar lander companies, satellite imaging firms, and space infrastructure providers.

VICR.headline: Here's Why Aehr Test Systems Popped Higher Today
VICR.description: Aehr Test Systems stock surged 16.2% following Vicor's unexpected raise in second-quarter revenue guidance. Vicor's strength in AI infrastructure power systems signals accelerating demand for data center solutions, which benefits Aehr's wafer-level burn-in test equipment business. Aehr recently received a $41 million order from a hyperscaler customer, bringing total orders to over $92 million for the second half of fiscal 2026, exceeding previous guidance.

MU.headline: Micron Hits $1 Trillion, Market Indexes Can't Agree on Much Else
MU.description: Micron Technology surged 18% after UBS tripled its price target to $1,625, pushing the memory-chip maker past $1 trillion in market capitalization for the first time. The rally boosted the Nasdaq and S&P 500, while the Dow declined due to falling oil prices from Iran negotiations. Despite Micron's modest index weighting, it contributed more to market movement than major tech giants.

MU.headline: This AI ETF's Price Nearly Doubled in a Few Weeks. Now Wall Street Wants to Add Leverage.
MU.description: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) has surged roughly 90% since its April launch, attracting $10 billion in assets by targeting AI memory and storage. Wall Street is now filing for a 2X leveraged version, but experts warn that leveraged ETFs carry significant risks including volatility decay, high fees, and are unsuitable for long-term investors, particularly in concentrated tech portfolios.

MU.headline: Nasdaq 100 Hits Record Above 30,000 On Micron's 18% Rally: Stock Market Today
MU.description: The Nasdaq 100 crossed 30,000 during intraday trading as semiconductor stocks surged, led by Micron Technology's 18% gain following a UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625. The rally was driven by increased AI spending expectations from hyperscalers, with chip stocks dominating market leadership. The S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while the Russell 2000 outperformed with a 1.7% gain. AutoZone plunged 10.1% after missing revenue estimates despite beating EPS.

MU.headline: Stock Market Today, May 26: Micron Surges After UBS Lifts Price Target on AI Optimism
MU.description: Micron Technology surged approximately 18% after UBS tripled its price target to $1,625, pushing the semiconductor maker toward a $1 trillion valuation. The rally was fueled by AI optimism and President Trump's praise for the company's U.S. investments. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq climbed on AI strength, while the Dow lagged as non-tech blue chips underperformed. Travel stocks like United Airlines and Carnival also rallied on easing oil prices and Iran peace talk optimism.

MU.headline: US Stocks Show AI Breadth Is Replacing Defensive Leadership
MU.description: US equities rallied post-Memorial Day with strong AI and small-cap leadership, as de-escalation hopes in the US-Iran conflict drove oil prices lower. Micron Technology surged 18% to cross $1 trillion market cap on a UBS price target hike, while quantum computing and space stocks rallied on government funding and SpaceX IPO anticipation. The Nasdaq rose 1.11% and Russell 2000 jumped 1.74%, though the Dow declined 0.19% as defensive sectors unwound.

MU.headline: Micron Stock Hits Record High After UBS More Than Triples Target: What Traders Are Watching Next
MU.description: UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri raised Micron's price target to $1,625 from $535, citing structural AI-driven demand and enhanced long-term agreements in the memory market. The analyst expects EPS to remain above $100 through 2029, marking a shift from traditional memory-cycle dynamics to an AI-demand story. Micron stock rose 13.71% to $853.98 on the news, up 189% year-to-date.

MU.headline: Why Marvell Technology Stock Just Popped
MU.description: Marvell Technology stock surged 8.4% on Tuesday following positive sentiment in the semiconductor sector. Mizuho's bullish outlook on Micron, citing strong AI-driven demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) with predicted 30-50% undersupply through 2027, boosted the sector. Marvell benefits directly as it collaborates with Micron on custom HBM architecture. Additionally, Marvell received three price target hikes and an upgrade to 'buy' from HSBC, which tripled its price target to $300 and raised f

MU.headline: The Next Threat To Nvidia Isn't A Chipmaker — It's An IPO
MU.description: Nvidia's dominance in AI infrastructure may face a new competitive threat not from rival chipmakers, but from potential IPOs of major AI application companies like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic. These companies could compete for investor capital rather than customers, forcing fund managers to choose between allocating capital to current AI infrastructure winners versus emerging AI application leaders.

MU.headline: 7 Small-Cap Stocks More Than Doubled In May: Micron Did Only 46%
MU.description: Seven Russell 2000 small-cap stocks more than doubled in May 2026, significantly outperforming Micron Technology's 46% gain. The rallies were driven by specific catalysts including strong earnings reports, guidance raises, strategic partnerships, and defense contracts. Hyliion Holdings led with a 213.6% gain, followed by Agilon Health at 209.1%, with each company benefiting from positive business developments and analyst upgrades.

MU.headline: Missed Out on the Initial AI Rally? These 3 AI Stocks Are Just Getting Started.
MU.description: Despite the strong AI sector rally since April, three stocks still offer significant upside potential: Meta Platforms trades at a discount to the S&P 500 with 33% revenue growth; Micron is capitalizing on memory chip shortages with expected 193% revenue growth this year; and Nebius is experiencing explosive growth with its AI-focused neocloud platform, projecting 550% growth this year.

FLY.headline: Firefly Aerospace Stock Soars On SpaceX IPO And Starship V3 Launch
FLY.description: Firefly Aerospace (FLY) shares surged 24.06% following SpaceX's successful Starship V3 test flight and the company's IPO filing seeking up to $75 billion at a potential $2 trillion valuation. The test demonstrated all 33 Raptor engines firing, successful space deployment, and heat shield separation. SpaceX's IPO prospectus, expected to list on Nasdaq around June 12 under ticker SPCX, has boosted broader space sector sentiment.

OUST.headline: Ouster Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
OUST.description: Ouster (NASDAQ:OUST) stock surged to a 52-week high, up 16.77% to $43.24, following a strategic partnership announcement with ARGUS Interception GmbH to integrate its digital lidar technology into drone defense systems. The rally was further supported by the company's Rev8 operating system achieving sensor qualification for NVIDIA's DRIVE Hyperion platform. Technical indicators show bullish momentum with the MACD above its signal line, though analysts maintain a Buy rating with a $41.50 price ta

ARM.headline: Nvidia has a $200 Billion Warning for AMD and Intel Stock Investors
ARM.description: Nvidia is entering the standalone server CPU market with its next-generation Vera processor, projecting $20 billion in revenue by 2027 from a $200 billion total addressable market. Using Arm-based architecture, Nvidia could capture significant market share from AMD and Intel, who currently dominate the x86-based server CPU space. This expansion threatens AMD and Intel's lucrative data center CPU business as hyperscalers increasingly prefer Arm architecture for cost efficiency.

DELL.headline: Cloud-Flation: Dell’s On-Premise AI Bet Pays Off
DELL.description: A fundamental shift in AI infrastructure is underway as enterprises move from centralized cloud data centers to on-premise hardware due to unsustainable token consumption costs (320x increase). Dell Technologies is positioned to dominate this transition through its AI Factory partnership with NVIDIA, having secured over 5,000 enterprise customers with 1,000 acquired in Q1 FY2027. The company's $43 billion backlog and 95% year-to-date stock surge reflect strong momentum, though valuation concerns

DELL.headline: These Are the AI Trades I'd Make Today if I Were Starting My Portfolio From Scratch
DELL.description: An investment analyst recommends three AI stocks for a fresh portfolio: Qualcomm for its edge AI and data center chip opportunities, Dell for its AI-optimized server infrastructure business, and ServiceNow for its workflow automation software with strong growth and profitability despite recent stock weakness.

DELL.headline: Five Things Moving Markets: Records, Record Lows, And $100 Oil All In One Week
DELL.description: Markets hit record highs with the S&P 500 posting its eighth straight weekly gain and the Dow closing at 50,579.70, while consumer sentiment fell to record lows. Oil prices remain elevated above $103 amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Fed officials signal no near-term rate cuts, with Waller calling rate-cut talk 'crazy.' Dell and HP surged on AI server demand, while SpaceX filed for IPO with major institutional interest.

APLD.headline: BABCOCK & WILCOX CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors with Large Losses to Contact the Firm
APLD.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims the company's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings stood on both sides of the deal and that Applied Digital may not have actually needed the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition and overstated business prospects. INFQ.headline: The U.S. Government Just Bet $2 Billion on 9 Quantum Computing Companies. Here's What It Means for Investors.
INFQ.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce announced $2.01 billion in CHIPS Act funding for nine quantum computing companies, with IBM receiving $1 billion for a new quantum chip foundry. While IBM stock jumped 12%, smaller quantum companies like D-Wave, Rigetti, and Infleqtion surged over 30% despite minimal revenues and significant losses.
